req.title: Device data ingest (boundary)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-047 mandates three distinct test axes: (1) happy-path ingest from a registered device (EP – valid partition), (2) rejection of an unregistered/invalid-cert device (NEG), and (3) boundary values on timestamp and numeric elements (BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from unregistered→registered to assert the device must be in the correct state before ingest succeeds (STC). A fifth decision-table test exercises the cross-product of cert validity × device registration to confirm all rejection paths are covered (DT). Together these five cases achieve the minimum required coverage of positive, negative, and boundary kinds while applying all five mandated heuristics.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate
actions: Construct a minimal FHIR Observation resource with a typical numeric value and current ISO-8601 timestamp
POST payload to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
expected: Response body contains an id field
Stored Observation is retrievable via GET /Observation/{id}
Numeric value is normalised per server-side rules
trace: R-HLT-047: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite.local"

@pytest.fixture
def registered_device_session():
    return <fixture_registered_device_session>

def test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation(registered_device_session):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-01T12:00:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72.0, "unit": "beats/min"}
    }
    resp = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    obs_id = body["id"]
    get_resp = registered_device_session.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Observation/{obs_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored["valueQuantity"]["value"] == <expected_normalised_value>(72.0)

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_with_401
preconditions: Device certificate is invalid or belongs to an unregistered device
FHIR Observation payload is otherwise schema-valid
actions: Construct a valid FHIR Observation payload
POST payload to /Ingest using an invalid/unregistered device certificate
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome with severity error
trace: R-HLT-047: unregistered devices shall be rejected

tests.name: test_boundary_timestamp_max_precision_and_min_numeric
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Device holds a valid mTLS certificate
actions: Construct Observation with effectiveDateTime at maximum sub-second precision (nanoseconds boundary)
Set valueQuantity to the system-defined minimum positive numeric (0.000001)
expected: Stored Observation timestamp is normalised without truncation error
Stored numeric value equals the minimum positive value after normalisation
trace: R-HLT-047: Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite.local"
MIN_POSITIVE_VALUE = 0.000001

@pytest.fixture
def registered_device_session():
    return <fixture_registered_device_session>

def test_boundary_timestamp_max_precision_and_min_numeric(registered_device_session):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-01T23:59:59.999999999Z",
        "valueQuantity": {"value": MIN_POSITIVE_VALUE, "unit": "mg/dL"}
    }
    resp = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    obs_id = resp.json()["id"]
    stored = registered_device_session.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Observation/{obs_id}").json()
    assert "effectiveDateTime" in stored
    assert stored["valueQuantity"]["value"] == pytest.approx(MIN_POSITIVE_VALUE, rel=1e-6)

tests.name: test_state_transition_device_registers_then_ingests
preconditions: Device exists in the system but is in UNREGISTERED state
actions: Attempt ingest while device is UNREGISTERED — expect 401
Register the device via /Device registration endpoint
Attempt ingest again with same payload — expect 201
expected: Second POST returns 201 and observation is stored
trace: R-HLT-047: A registered device shall push Observations

tests.name: test_decision_table_cert_validity_x_registration_status
preconditions: Four device configurations prepared: (valid cert + registered), (valid cert + unregistered), (invalid cert + registered), (invalid cert + unregistered)
actions: POST a valid Observation to /Ingest for each of the four device configurations
trace: R-HLT-047: registered device shall push; unregistered shall be rejected
